name: Maggie’s Quick & Easy Gazpacho
intro: Sometimes, strangers become lifelong family. Barbara was my mom’s best friend from college, and my mom made her my godmother when I was seven years old. I’m so happy she did. Barbara has always been so special to me. She is thoughtful, caring, fun, unique and listens so well. I always feel comforted and supported by her. Barbara’s love language is gift-giving, and since being in my life, she has never missed out on a birthday or holiday without getting me something. She always seems to know what I like, and she makes the best shopping partner. I remember our trips to the mall and blasting music loudly in the car while we traveled to a fun event or outing. Barbara felt more like my best friend, and honestly, she is just someone you must meet to understand how special she really is.
method: Barbara’s mom, Maggie, has always been an incredible cook. After eating most of her meals, I would ask for tips and advice on how to make them. Maggie is not only a great cook, she is funny and creative. Maggie made me the best gazpacho I ever had in my life. Puerto Rican gazpacho is a mix of green banana, yuca, bacalao (salt fish), onion, avocado, olives and olive oil. It’s pretty much like a savory salad, and it is so easy to make. Thank you, Maggie, for feeding me every time I was over. Thank you, Barbara, for accepting me into your world.
Peel your yuca and green bananas and cut them into ½-inch (1.3-cm) pieces.
In a large pot, bring water to a boil. Once boiling, add your yuca and green banana with about 1 tablespoon (18 g) of salt and cook for 15 minutes, or until tender. Drain and let cool.

On a cutting board, roughly chop the hearts of palm into bacalao-like pieces (small and oval, almost like anchovies). They don’t have to look perfect! Season with a pinch of salt and the nori flakes to give them a hint of sea flavor that mimics salt fish.
Once the yuca and banana are cool, transfer them to a bowl and add the hearts of palm, onion, olives, avocado, olive oil, red wine vinegar, adobo, garlic powder and pepper, and mix.
The gazpacho is complete, and you can eat it with white rice or by itself.
